phrases.intent_en: Ask how to get to Manneken Pis from the Grand-Place
phrases.text_local: Comment aller au Manneken Pis depuis la Grand-Place ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MON ah-LAY oh mah-nuh-KEEN PEES duh-PWEE lah grahn PLAHS
phrases.cultural_note: Manneken Pis is a short walk from the Grand-Place. It's smaller than most visitors expect.

responses.english: Go down Rue de l'Étuve, it's three minutes away.
responses.phonetic: day-sahn-DAY lah roo duh lay-TOOV, say ah trwah mee-NOOT
responses.french: Descendez la rue de l'Étuve, c'est à trois minutes.

phrases.cultural_note: Manneken Pis has over 1,000 costumes, changed several times per week. Tuesday at 2pm is the regular costume change — check the schedule.

responses.english: Several times a week. The schedule is posted nearby.
responses.phonetic: plu-ZYUHR fwah par suh-MEN. loh-REHR eh ah-fee-SHAY ah koh-TAY
responses.french: Plusieurs fois par semaine. L'horaire est affiché à côté.

phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il un musée à proximité ?
phrases.cultural_note: The Museum of Brussels ( Maison du Roi) is nearby and houses the original statue and many of its costumes. Allow 30-45 minutes for a visit.

responses.english: Yes, the City Museum on the Grand-Place, with the costumes.
responses.phonetic: wee, luh myoo-ZAY duh lah VEEL soor lah grahn PLAHS, ah-VEK lay kos-TOOM
responses.french: Oui, le Musée de la Ville sur la Grand-Place, avec les costumes.

phrases.cultural_note: A female version, Jeanneken Pis, exists on the Rue des Huiliers — a smaller, less famous statue of a girl urinating.

responses.english: Yes, there is a female version called Jeanneken Pis, located on Rue des Huiliers.
responses.french: Oui, il y a une version féminine appelée Jeanneken Pis, située rue des Huiliers.
